text:
Man-faced Apes (, Jinmenzaru) are a race of carnivorous primates capable of disguising themselves as humans and mimicking human speech. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 8 

Appearance

Aside from their heads which differ from each other, the Man-faced Apes all have fangs, and frail thin bodies, and are covered in fur, excluding their hands and wrist area, pecs, abdomen, back area, calves, and feet. They also have long sharp claws and toenails. 

Behavior

Man-faced Apes are known for being deceitful towards human beings. They often lure humans into the Milsy Wetlands to devour them, and allow other creatures to kill the prey due to their lack of body strength. 

Background

They reside in Milsy Wetlands. They use their deceiving appearance and abilities to lure unsuspecting humans into the foggy wetlands and, when their guard is down, the Man-faced Ape and other animals attack and will proceed to devour the humans as they love the taste of fresh human flesh. They resort to this tactic due to their weak bodies, and often attack in large numbers when their prey's guards are down. 

Plot

Hunter Exam arc

The Man-faced Ape is first seen by the 287th Hunter Exam participants after completing the first part of the first phase of the Hunter Exam, i.e the marathon. Two Man-faced Apes were disguised as Satotz, the examiner at that stage, and another man claiming to be the real Hunter examiner, in order to trick the applicants into thinking that Satotz was not the real examiner, and lure the applicants to devour them. Hisoka tests Satotz and the Man-faced Apes by throwing cards at them. Satotz catches the cards easily while the Man-faced Apes are killed. 

Trivia

Since the ability to talk is a distinctive trait of Magical Beasts, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 4 it is possible that Man-faced Apes are counted as such, also keeping their human-like intelligence into account.

Anime and Manga Differences

In the manga and 1999 anime adaptation, Hisoka kills both Man-faced Apes, Hunter × Hunter - Episode 8 (1999) while in the 2011 anime adaptation, he only kills one of them. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 4 (2011) 
A Man-faced Ape with the appearance of Satotz makes an appearance during the 2nd phase of the Hunter Exam. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 9 (1999)
